name: the-oracle
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "use the oracle" or "ask the oracle" for deep research, analysis, or architectural questions. The oracle excels at multi-source research combining codebase exploration and web searches, then synthesizing findings into actionable answers. Use for complex questions requiring investigation across multiple sources, architectural analysis, refactoring plans, debugging mysteries, and code reviews.
capabilities: Dispatches a dedicated research agent using the Task tool
Conducts multi-source investigation combining codebase exploration and web searches
Traces code paths, call graphs, and dependencies
Synthesizes findings from logs, documentation, and code into coherent answers
author: Cygnusfear
securitySummary: All 20 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation-only guidance containing no actual code. Detected SHA256 hashes were misidentified as weak crypto (SHA256 is secure). GitHub repository URLs and hash values in metadata triggered network/C2 false positives. Backtick-wrapped code examples in markdown were misidentified as shell execution. No risk factors present.
valueStatement: Complex research and analysis questions require investigation across multiple sources. The Oracle skill dispatches a dedicated research agent to explore codebases, search documentation, trace call graphs, and synthesize findings into actionable answers.
userTitle: Use the Oracle for Deep Research
limitations: Does not execute code directly, only dispatches a research agent
Relies on user-provided context and success criteria
Cannot take action on findings, only report them

useCases.targetUser: Senior developers and architects evaluating system design
useCases.title: Architectural Analysis
useCases.description: Analyze codebase architecture to identify problems and design improvements. The oracle traces component relationships and dependencies to recommend refactoring steps.

useCases.title: Debugging Investigation
useCases.description: Investigate root causes of bugs by correlating code paths with log files. The oracle traces execution flow and identifies error conditions.

useCases.title: Comprehensive Code Review
useCases.description: Review code for correctness, edge cases, performance issues, and security concerns. The oracle examines the full context of changes.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Oracle Query
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick research questions with minimal context

promptTemplates.title: Architectural Review
Debugging Session
promptTemplates.scenario: Investigating bugs with logs and code traces

promptTemplates.title: Full Code Review
promptTemplates.scenario: Detailed code review with full context

bestPractices: Provide specific success criteria so the oracle knows when the answer is complete
Include all relevant context: file paths, error messages, and what has already been tried
Ask for specific evidence like file paths and line numbers to verify findings
antiPatterns: Vague questions without clear success criteria waste research cycles
Asking the oracle to fix issues instead of reviewing them first
Providing incomplete context and expecting the oracle to guess missing details

faq.question: When should I use the oracle instead of regular Claude?
faq.answer: Use the oracle for complex questions requiring deep investigation across multiple sources. Regular Claude is better for straightforward coding tasks. The oracle excels at research, analysis, and tracing through unfamiliar codebases.

faq.question: How long does an oracle investigation take?
faq.answer: The oracle uses extended thinking to thoroughly investigate. Complex questions may take longer but produce more comprehensive answers with supporting evidence.

faq.question: Can the oracle execute code?
faq.answer: No. The oracle is a research agent that analyzes existing code, logs, and documentation. It does not write or execute code itself.

faq.question: What makes a good oracle question?
faq.answer: Good questions have: a clear core question, relevant context like files or error messages, and specific success criteria defining what a satisfactory answer looks like.

faq.question: Does the oracle search the web?
faq.answer: Yes. The oracle can use WebSearch for external documentation when needed, combining web research with codebase exploration for comprehensive answers.

faq.question: How does the oracle differ from Task tool?
faq.answer: The oracle is a skill that provides a template for using the Task tool effectively. It structures how to formulate research prompts with phases for planning, execution, synthesis, and delivery.
